11.1: Arjuna said: Through the compassionate teaching you have given about the soul and the deeper nature of existence, my confusion has begun to dissolve.
11.2: You have explained the origin and dissolution of life, and the enduring greatness behind the universe.
11.3: Everything you have said about the deeper reality is true. I now wish to see this reality in its full and universal form.
11.4: If you believe I am capable of seeing it, reveal to me that imperishable form of existence.
11.5: Krishna said: Behold the countless expressions of existence, appearing in many forms, colors, and shapes.
11.6: See the many powers of nature and the forces that sustain the universe. Witness wonders never seen before.
11.7: In this moment you will see the entire universe gathered together in one presence, with everything that exists contained within it.
11.8: But this cannot be perceived with ordinary vision. I grant you a deeper sight so that you may perceive this reality.
11.9: Sanjaya said: Having spoken these words, Krishna revealed to Arjuna a vast and universal vision.
11.10: It appeared with countless faces and eyes, filled with astonishing forms and symbols of power.
11.11: It shone with radiant beauty and immeasurable presence, extending in every direction.
11.12: Its brilliance was like the light of a thousand suns rising at once in the sky.
11.13: Within that vision, Arjuna saw the entire universe gathered together in a single presence.
11.14: Overwhelmed with awe, Arjuna bowed deeply and spoke with folded hands.
11.15: Arjuna said: I see all beings and all powers of existence within you. I see creators, sages, and the countless forms of life.
11.16: I see you extending in every direction with endless forms. I see no beginning, middle, or end.
11.17: Your radiance shines everywhere like blazing fire or the sun itself.
11.18: You are the imperishable reality, the highest truth to be known, and the ultimate foundation of the universe.
11.19: You appear without beginning or end, infinite in power, illuminating the entire universe.
11.20: All space between heaven and earth is filled with this presence, and the worlds tremble before its immensity.
11.21: The powers of the universe approach in reverence. Some praise it with humility, while others stand in awe.
11.22: All forces of nature and realms of existence gaze upon this vision in astonishment.
11.23: Seeing this vast and overwhelming form, the worlds tremble, and so do I.
11.24: Your blazing presence fills the sky with color and fire. My heart trembles and I find no peace.
11.25: Your fearful power burns like the fire of destruction. I lose my sense of direction and feel overwhelmed.
11.26: I see the warriors and kings of both armies rushing toward this immense force.
11.27: They are drawn into its blazing power, as if pulled toward their destiny.
11.28: Just as rivers rush toward the ocean, these warriors move inevitably toward this great force.
11.29: Like moths rushing into flame, they move toward destruction.
11.30: Your blazing presence consumes everything, filling the universe with its overwhelming radiance.
11.31: Tell me who you are in this terrible form. I wish to understand your true nature and purpose.
11.32: Krishna said: I am time, the force that brings all things to their end. The warriors before you are already destined to fall.
11.33: Therefore arise and act. These outcomes are already unfolding within the movement of time. You are simply an instrument through which events take place.
11.34: The warriors you fear to confront are already caught in the unfolding of destiny. Stand and fulfill your role.
11.35: Sanjaya said: Hearing these words, Arjuna trembled and bowed deeply once again.
11.36: Arjuna said: It is right that the world rejoices in your greatness. Those who oppose truth flee, while the wise bow in reverence.
11.37: You are the source beyond all creators, the eternal presence beyond both existence and non-existence.
11.38: You are the original source of all life, the ultimate foundation of the universe, the one who knows and sustains all.
11.39: All the powers of nature arise from you. Again and again I bow before this infinite presence.
11.40: You exist everywhere, in every direction. Your presence fills all existence.
11.41: In the past I spoke to you casually as a friend, not understanding the depth of what you truly represent.
11.42: If I have ever shown disrespect in play or conversation, forgive me for not recognizing your true nature.
11.43: You are the source and teacher of the universe. Nothing can equal the power that sustains all existence.
11.44: With humility I bow before you and seek your grace, as a child seeks forgiveness from a parent or a friend from a friend.
11.45: Though I am amazed by what I have seen, the vision also fills me with fear. Please return to the form that feels familiar and gentle.
11.46: Let me see you again as before, in the form I recognize.
11.47: Krishna said: Out of grace I revealed this universal vision to you. No one before you has seen it in this way.
11.48: This vision cannot be reached through rituals, study, charity, or severe discipline alone.
11.49: Do not be afraid of what you have seen. Let your mind return to calm and behold once again the familiar form.
11.50: Sanjaya said: Krishna then returned to his gentle form and reassured the frightened Arjuna.
11.51: Arjuna said: Seeing your familiar human form again, my mind is at peace.
11.52: Krishna said: This universal vision is extremely difficult to perceive. Even the highest beings long to see it.
11.53: It cannot be reached through study, rituals, charity, or sacrifice alone.
11.54: But through wholehearted devotion and sincere alignment with truth, it can be truly known and experienced.
11.55: One who acts with dedication to truth, who sees it as the highest goal, who lives without attachment and without hostility toward any being, ultimately comes into harmony with it.
14.1: Krishna says he will again explain the highest knowledge, the most profound understanding, by which sages have reached the highest realization.
14.2: Those who understand this knowledge come to share in the deeper nature of reality and move beyond the cycles of creation and dissolution.
14.3: The great source of existence is Brahman (the ultimate reality), within which the seed of life gives rise to all beings.
14.4: All forms that appear in the world arise from Brahman (the ultimate reality), the universal field from which life emerges.
14.5: Three qualities of Prakriti (nature) bind the soul to embodied life: sattva (clarity), rajas (activity), and tamas (inertia).
14.6: Sattva (clarity) is pure and luminous. It brings harmony and understanding but still binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
14.7: Rajas (activity) arises from craving and restless desire. It binds the soul through constant involvement in action.
14.8: Tamas (inertia), born from ignorance, clouds understanding and binds through laziness, confusion, and neglect.
14.9: Sattva (clarity) binds through attachment to happiness, rajas (activity) through attachment to action, and tamas (inertia) through confusion and neglect.
14.10: At times sattva (clarity) prevails, overcoming the others; at other times rajas (activity) or tamas (inertia) become dominant.
14.11: When understanding shines through the senses and mind, it is a sign that sattva (clarity) has grown strong.
14.12: Restlessness, ambition, effort, and longing appear when rajas (activity) becomes dominant.
14.13: Darkness, laziness, confusion, and delusion arise when tamas (inertia) prevails.
14.14: If the soul leaves the body while sattva (clarity) is dominant, it moves toward realms of greater understanding.
14.15: When death occurs under rajas (activity), the soul returns among those driven by action; under tamas (inertia), it returns among those clouded by ignorance.
14.16: Actions rooted in sattva (clarity) lead toward harmony. Actions born of rajas (activity) lead to dissatisfaction, and those of tamas (inertia) deepen ignorance.
14.17: From sattva (clarity) arises knowledge. From rajas (activity) arises craving. From tamas (inertia) arise confusion and delusion.
14.18: Those grounded in sattva (clarity) rise toward higher understanding. Those ruled by rajas (activity) remain caught in striving. Those ruled by tamas (inertia) sink into ignorance.
14.19: When one sees that the qualities of Prakriti (nature) alone drive the movements of life and recognizes the reality beyond them, true freedom begins.
14.20: Transcending these three qualities frees the soul from birth, death, and sorrow, revealing its immortal nature.
14.21: Arjuna asks how one can recognize a person who has gone beyond these three qualities and how such freedom is achieved.
14.22: Krishna explains that such a person neither resists nor clings to the movements of clarity, activity, or inertia as they arise and pass.
14.23: Remaining steady and detached, they recognize that the qualities of Prakriti (nature) simply move within life.
14.24: They remain balanced in pleasure and pain, seeing wealth and poverty, praise and blame with equal clarity.
14.25: They remain the same in honor or insult, friend or enemy, free from ego-driven pursuits.
14.26: One who lives with unwavering dedication to truth rises beyond these qualities and becomes capable of realizing Brahman (the ultimate reality).
14.27: For Brahman (the ultimate reality) is the foundation of immortality, enduring truth, and lasting peace.
15.1: Krishna describes existence as an upside-down tree, whose roots lie in Brahman (the ultimate reality) while its branches extend into the world of experience.
15.2: Its branches grow through the qualities of Prakriti (nature) and extend through sensory desires, binding beings to cycles of action.
15.3: Its true form cannot be clearly perceived. Only by cutting through attachment can one free oneself from its grip.
15.4: Then one seeks the highest state of existence from which life originally flows and to which the awakened soul returns.
15.5: Those free from pride and confusion, who have overcome attachment and remain steady in the soul, move toward that imperishable state.
15.6: That realm is beyond the light of sun, moon, or fire. Having reached it, one does not return to the cycles of the world.
15.7: Each living being carries an eternal spark of Brahman (the ultimate reality), expressing itself through mind and senses within Prakriti (nature).
15.8: As the wind carries fragrance from place to place, the soul carries the mind and senses when it enters and leaves a body.
15.9: Through the senses and mind, the soul experiences sight, sound, touch, taste, and smell.
15.10: Those lost in confusion fail to see this inner presence moving through life, but those with clear understanding perceive it.
15.11: Those who seek with discipline perceive the soul within themselves, while those lacking awareness fail to see it even when they strive.
15.12: The light that shines in the sun and illuminates the world, and the light within the moon and fire, arise from Brahman (the ultimate reality).
15.13: The sustaining power within the earth nourishes all life, and the cycles of nature provide the energy that sustains plants and living beings.
15.14: The life-force within every body transforms food into energy and sustains the living organism.
15.15: Within every heart resides the source from which memory, knowledge, and even forgetfulness arise. The ancient teachings ultimately point toward Brahman (the ultimate reality).
15.16: In existence there are two aspects: the changing and the unchanging. All living forms belong to the changing realm, while the soul remains constant.
15.17: Beyond both stands the highest reality, the supreme presence that sustains all worlds.
15.18: Because this reality transcends both the changing and the unchanging, it is known as the highest Self.
15.19: One who understands this truth clearly comes to understand the essence of existence and lives in alignment with Brahman (the ultimate reality).
15.20: Krishna concludes that this teaching reveals a profound secret: one who understands it becomes truly wise and fulfills the deepest purpose of life.
